name: Lo Mein
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Chinese egg noodles (dried, not fresh)
2 ounces Raw lean pork, cut into matchsticks
2 ounces Raw lean beef, cut into similar-sized pieces
2 ounces Raw chicken, cut into small pieces of any shape
2 ounces Raw shelled shrimp, cooked if available
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the noodles and cook for 3 minutes. Drain and toss with sesame oil and regular oil; set aside.
2. In four separate dishes, mix each meat with half a teaspoon of dry sherry and half a teaspoon of cornstarch.
3. Heat the oil in a skillet or wok over high heat. Add the meats one at a time, stirring for a few seconds between each addition: pork, beef, chicken, shrimp. As soon as you've added and tossed the shrimp, add the mushrooms and cabbage. Cook until the cabbage is wilted.
4. Add scallions, soy sauce, salt, and MSG (or chicken bouillon). Stir to combine.
5. Add the noodles and bean sprouts, and stir-fry for 3 minutes. Serve hot on a warm platter.
